subject: General Mathematics / Mathematics (Core)
aims_of_the_syllabus: Understanding of mathematical concepts and their relationship to the acquisition of entrepreneurial skills for everyday living in the global world
Ability to translate problems into mathematical language and solve them using appropriate methods
Ability to be accurate to a degree relevant to the problem at hand

examination_scheme.description: There will be two papers, Papers 1 and 2, both of which must be taken
examination_scheme.paper_1: Will consist of fifty multiple-choice objective questions, drawn from the common areas of the syllabus, to be answered in 1.5 hours for 50 marks

paper_2.description: Will consist of thirteen essay questions in two sections - Sections A and B, to be answered in 2.5 hours for 100 marks. Candidates will be required to answer ten questions in all
paper_2.section_a: Will consist of five compulsory questions, elementary in nature carrying a total of 40 marks. The questions will be drawn from the common areas of the syllabus
paper_2.section_b: Will consist of eight questions of greater length and difficulty. The questions shall include a maximum of two which shall be drawn from parts of the syllabuses which may not be peculiar to candidates' home countries. Candidates will be expected to answer five questions for 60 marks

number_and_numeration.contents: Conversion of numbers from one base to another; Basic operations on number bases
number_and_numeration.notes: Conversion from one base to base 10 and vice versa. Conversion from one base to another base. Addition, subtraction and multiplication of number bases

number_and_numeration.contents: Concept of Modulo Arithmetic; Addition, subtraction and multiplication operations in modulo arithmetic; Application to daily life
number_and_numeration.notes: Interpretation of modulo arithmetic e.g. 6 + 4 = k (mod 7), 3 x 5 = b (mod 6), m = 2 (mod 3), etc. Relate to market days, clock, shift duty, etc

number_and_numeration.contents: Basic operations on fractions and decimals; Approximations and significant figures
number_and_numeration.notes: Approximations should be realistic e.g. a road is not measured correct to the nearest cm

number_and_numeration.contents: Laws of indices; Numbers in standard form (scientific notation)
number_and_numeration.notes: Include negative and fractional indices. Expression of large and small numbers in standard form e.g. 375300000 = 3.753 x 10^8, 0.00000035 = 3.5 x 10^-7

number_and_numeration.contents: Relationship between indices and logarithms; Basic rules of logarithms; Use of tables of logarithms and antilogarithms
number_and_numeration.notes: Calculations involving multiplication, division, powers and roots. Use of tables of squares, square roots and reciprocals is accepted

number_and_numeration.contents: Patterns of sequences; Arithmetic progression (A.P.); Geometric Progression (G.P.)
number_and_numeration.notes: Determine any term of a given sequence. Simple cases only, including word problems (Include sum for A.P. and exclude sum for G.P.)

number_and_numeration.contents: Idea of sets, universal sets, finite and infinite sets, subsets, empty sets and disjoint sets. Union, intersection and complement of sets; Solution of practical problems using Venn diagrams
number_and_numeration.notes: Use of Venn diagrams restricted to at most 3 sets

number_and_numeration.contents: Simple statements; True and false statements; Negation of statements, implications
number_and_numeration.notes: Use of symbols and Venn diagrams

number_and_numeration.topic: Positive and negative integers, rational numbers
number_and_numeration.contents: The four basic operations on rational numbers
number_and_numeration.notes: Match rational numbers with points on the number line. Notation: Natural numbers (N), Integers (Z), Rational numbers (Q)

number_and_numeration.contents: Simplification and rationalization of simple surds; Basic operations on surds
number_and_numeration.notes: Surds of the form a / sqrt(b), a*sqrt(b) and a + b where a is a rational number and b is a positive integer. Basic operations on surds

number_and_numeration.contents: Identification of order, notation and types of matrices; Addition, subtraction, scalar multiplication and multiplication of matrices; Determinant of a matrix
number_and_numeration.notes: Not more than 3x3 matrices. Restrict to 2x2 matrices for operations, determinants, and simultaneous linear equations

number_and_numeration.contents: Ratio between two similar quantities; Proportion between two or more similar quantities; Financial partnerships, rates of work, costs, taxes, foreign exchange, density, mass, distance, time and speed
number_and_numeration.notes: Relate to real life situations. Include average rates, taxes e.g. VAT, Withholding tax, etc

number_and_numeration.contents: Simple interest, commission, discount, depreciation, profit and loss, compound interest, hire purchase and percentage error
number_and_numeration.notes: Limit compound interest to a maximum of 3 years
Calculation of depreciation on fixed assets, amortization on capitalized assets, simple problems on annuities, and shares/stocks, debentures, bonds

number_and_numeration.contents: Direct, inverse, partial and joint variations
number_and_numeration.notes: Expression of various types of variation in mathematical symbols and application to simple practical problems

algebraic_processes.contents: Formulating algebraic expressions from given situations; Evaluation of algebraic expressions

algebraic_processes.notes: Factorization of expressions like ax + ay, a^2 - b^2, ax^2 + bx + c. Carry out binary operations on real numbers

algebraic_processes.contents: Linear equations in one variable; Simultaneous linear equations in two variables
algebraic_processes.notes: Solving by elimination, substitution and graphical methods. Word problems involving one or two variables

algebraic_processes.contents: Change of subject of a formula/relation; Substitution
algebraic_processes.notes: Finding the value of a variable given the values of other variables

algebraic_processes.contents: Solution of quadratic equations; Forming quadratic equation with given roots; Application of solution of quadratic equation in practical problems
algebraic_processes.notes: Using factorization, completing the square, and use of formula. Simple rational roots only

algebraic_processes.topic: Graphs of Linear and Quadratic functions
algebraic_processes.contents: Interpretation of graphs, coordinate of points, table of values, drawing quadratic graphs and obtaining roots; Graphical solution of a pair of equations; Drawing tangents to curves to determine gradient
algebraic_processes.notes: Finding coordinates of maximum/minimum points, intercepts, axis of symmetry, and solving related equations

algebraic_processes.contents: Solution of linear inequalities in one variable; Graphical solution of linear inequalities in two variables; Graphical solution of simultaneous linear inequalities in two variables
algebraic_processes.notes: Application to real life situations e.g. minimum cost, maximum profit, linear programming

algebraic_processes.contents: Operations on algebraic fractions with monomial and binomial denominators; Values for which a fraction is undefined
Types of Functions; Functions as a mapping, determination of the rule of a given mapping/function

mensuration.contents: Use of Pythagoras theorem, sine and cosine rules to determine lengths and distances; Lengths of arcs of circles, perimeters of sectors and segments; Longitudes and Latitudes
mensuration.notes: Distances along latitudes and longitudes and their corresponding angles

mensuration.contents: Triangles and special quadrilaterals; Circles, sectors and segments of circles; Surface areas of cubes, cuboids, cylinder, pyramids, right triangular prisms, cones and spheres
mensuration.notes: Areas of similar figures, compound shapes, and relationship between sector of a circle and surface area of a cone

mensuration.contents: Volumes of cubes, cuboids, cylinders, cones, right pyramids and spheres; Volumes of similar solids

plane_geometry.contents: Angles at a point add up to 360 degrees; Adjacent angles on a straight line are supplementary; Vertically opposite angles are equal
plane_geometry.notes: Consider acute, obtuse, reflex angles, etc

plane_geometry.topic: Angles and intercepts on parallel lines
plane_geometry.contents: Alternate angles are equal; Corresponding angles are equal; Interior opposite angles are supplementary; Intercept theorem
plane_geometry.notes: Application to proportional division of a line segment

plane_geometry.contents: Sum of angles of a triangle; Exterior angle of a triangle; Congruent triangles; Properties of special triangles and quadrilaterals; Properties of similar triangles; Sum of angles of a polygon; Exterior angles of a polygon; Parallelograms on the same base
plane_geometry.notes: Formal proofs of underlined topics may be required

plane_geometry.contents: Chords; Angles subtended by arcs and at the centre; Angle in a semicircle; Angles in same and opposite segments; Perpendicularity of tangent and radius; Alternate segment theorem
plane_geometry.notes: Formal proofs of underlined topics may be required

plane_geometry.contents: Bisectors of angles and line segments; Parallel or perpendicular lines; Standard angles; Triangles and quadrilaterals from sufficient data
plane_geometry.notes: Include combinations of standard angles e.g. 75, 105, 135 degrees

plane_geometry.contents: Knowledge of basic loci and their intersections in 2 dimensions

coordinate_geometry.contents: Concept of x-y plane; Coordinates of points; Midpoint and distance between two points; Gradient (slope) of a line; Equation of a line
coordinate_geometry.notes: Equations in the form y = mx + c and y - y_1 = m(x - x_1)

trigonometry.topic: Sine, Cosine and Tangent of an angle
trigonometry.contents: Sine, cosine and tangent of acute angles; Use of tables; Trigonometric ratios of 30, 45 and 60 degrees; Angles from 0 to 360 degrees; Graphs of sine and cosine; Graphs of trigonometric ratios
Calculating angles of elevation and depression; Application to heights and distances
Bearing of one point from another; Calculation of distances and angles
trigonometry.notes: Use of diagram required; Sine and cosine rules may be used

introductory_calculus.contents: Differentiation of algebraic functions; Integration of simple algebraic functions
introductory_calculus.notes: Application to maximum and minimum values, rates of change, distance/velocity/time, and evaluation of simple definite integrals

statistics_and_probability.contents: Frequency distribution; Pie charts, bar charts, histograms and frequency polygons; Mean, median and mode for discrete and grouped data; Cumulative frequency curve (Ogive); Measures of dispersion
statistics_and_probability.notes: Measures include range, inter-quartile range, variance, mean deviation and standard deviation

statistics_and_probability.contents: Experimental and theoretical probability; Addition of probabilities for mutually exclusive and independent events; Multiplication of probabilities for independent events
statistics_and_probability.notes: Simple practical problems only, including problems with and without replacement

vectors_and_transformation.contents: Vectors as directed line segments; Cartesian components; Magnitude, equal vectors, addition/subtraction, zero vector, parallel vectors, scalar multiplication
vectors_and_transformation.notes: Knowledge of graphical representation is necessary

vectors_and_transformation.contents: Reflection, rotation, translation, and enlargement of points and shapes
vectors_and_transformation.notes: Restrict plane to x and y axes, specific lines, origin rotations (-180 to 180 degrees), translation vectors, and scale factors

units.mass: 1000 milligrammes (mg) = 1 gramme (g). 1000 grammes (g) = 1 kilogramme (kg). 1000 kilogrammes (kg) = 1 tonne

other_important_information: Mathematics and Statistical tables published or approved by WAEC may be used in the examination room
The use of non-programmable, silent and cordless calculators is allowed without printout, receive/send capabilities
Candidates should bring rulers, pairs of compasses, protractors, set squares, etc. Graph papers ruled in 2mm squares will be provided
Some questions may prohibit the use of tables and/or calculators
